questions.body: Apples dropping from apple trees were observed by many people before Newton. But why they fall, was explained by Isaac Newton postulating the law of universal gravitation. Which of the following statements best describes the situation?

choices.a: The force of gravity acts only on the apple
choices.b:
The apple is attracted towards the
surface of the earth
choices.c:
Both earth and apple experience the same
force of attraction towards each other
choices.d:
Apple falls due to earthвҖҷs gravity and
hence only (a) is true and (c) is absurd

questions.solution:
According to Newton's law of gravitation,
force of gravitation acts on both interacting
object having same magnitude of force and
this force is attractive in nature.
questions.subject: physics

questions.body: Consider the motion of a small spherical steel body of mass m, falling freely through a long column of a fluid that opposes its motion with a force proportional to its speed. If weight mg, opposing force (Fv) and buoyant force (Fb) act on the body, then the correct equation relating these forces, after the terminal velocity is reached, is:

mg = Fv + Fb

questions.subject: physics

questions.body: A Physics teacher and his family are travelling in a car on a highway during a severe lightning storm. Choose the correct option:

choices.a:
Safest place will be inside the car as the
charges due to lightning tend to remain on
the metal sheet / skin of the vehicle if
struck by lightning.
choices.b:
It's too dangerous to be inside the car. As
the car has a metal body the charges tend
to accumulate on the surface and will
generate a strong electric field inside the
car.
choices.c:
Safest place is under a tree. It's better to
get drenched under a tree as the wet tree
will provide a path to the charges for
earthing.
choices.d:
It is safer to exit the car and stand on
open ground

questions.solution: By the concept of electrostatic shielding

(P) Sound wave is produced inside the string (Q) Sound wave in the string is transverse (R) Wavelength of the sound wave in surrounding air is equal to the wavelength of the transverse wave on the string (S) Loudness of sound is proportional to the square of the amplitude of the vibrating string Choose the correct option.

пҒң

questions.body: How many positive integers N give a remainder 8 when 2008 is divided by N?
questions.solution:
Here, remainder is 8
пҒң
N must be greater than 8
N must be factors of = 2008 вҖ“ 8 = 2000
    = 24 Г— 53
Total number of factors = 5 Г— 4 = 20
пҒң
Factors greater than 8 are 10, 16, 20, 25,
40, 50, 80, 100, 125, 200, 250, 400, 500,
1000, 2000.
So, total number of solutions = 15
Hence, option (d) is correct

|x| = вҖ“3

Product of roots = (+8) Г— (вҖ“8) = вҖ“64
Option (a) is correct.

questions.body: The number of triples (x, y, z) such that any one of these numbers is added to the product of the other two, the result is 2, is
questions.solution:
x + yz = 2
...(i)
y + zx = 2
...(ii)
z + xy = 2
...(iii)
Subtracting (ii) from (i),
(x вҖ“ y) = z(x вҖ“ y)
пҒң
Either x = y or z = 1
Case I :
If z = 1
пғһ
x + y = 2 and xy = 1
пҒң
пҖ«
пҖҪ
1
x
2
x
пғһ
x2 вҖ“ 2x + 1 = 0
пғһ
x = 1
пҒң
y = 1
пҒң
(1, 1, 1) is one of the triplet
Case II :
If x = y
пғһ
x(1 + z) = 2 and z + x2 = 2
пғһ
x + zx = z + x2
пғһ
x вҖ“ z = x(x вҖ“ z)
пғһ
Either x = z or x = 1
(i) If x = z пғһ x = y = z
пғһ
x + x2 = 2
пғһ
x = 1 or вҖ“ 2
пҒң
(вҖ“2, вҖ“2, вҖ“2) is second triplet

(ii) If x = 1 пғһ y = 1
пҒң
z = 1
пҒң
(1, 1, 1) is repeated triplet.
So only two triplets (1, 1, 1) and (вҖ“2, вҖ“2, вҖ“2)
are possible.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

has more than two roots, then this equation is
an identity.
пғһ
пҒЎ2 вҖ“ 5пҒЎ + 6 = 0, пҒЎ2 вҖ“ 3пҒЎ + 2 = 0 and
пҒЎ2 = 4
пғһ
пҒЎ = 2, 3 and пҒЎ = 1, 2 and пҒЎ = вҖ“2, 2
пҒң
Common value of пҒЎ is 2.
Option (a) is correct.
questions.subject: maths

questions.body: Mr. X with his eight children of different ages is on a family trip. His oldest child, who is 9 years old saw a license plate with a 4-digit number in which each of two digits appear two times. вҖңLook daddy!вҖқ she exclaims. вҖңThat number is evenly divisible by the age of each of us kids!вҖқ. вҖңThatвҖҷs right,вҖқ replies Mr. X, вҖңand the last two digits just happen to be my ageвҖқ. Which of the following is not the age of one of Mr. XвҖҷs children?
questions.solution:
Possible ages of children can be 1, 2, 3, 4, 5,
6, 7, 8, 9.
вҲө
There are 8 children and oldest child is 9
years old.
пҒң
Sum of four digits must be divisible by 9.
If four digit number is divisible by 9 then
possible combinations of digits must be
(0, 9), (1, 8), (2, 7), (3, 6), (4, 5)
Also the number should be even because
if number is not divisible by 2 then it
cannot be divisible by 4 and 8.
Hence age of the child cannot be 5
вҲө Even number is not divisible by 5 if last
    digit is non-zero.
If last digit is zero then only two
possibilities can occur.
99 00  is not possible because last two
digits are the age of his father.
99 90  is not divisible by 4 and 8 both
hence it is also not possible.
пҒң Option (b) is correct.
questions.subject: maths

questions.body: How many number lie between 11 and 1111 which divided by 9 leave a remainder 6 and when divided by 21 leave a remainder 12?
questions.solution:
Numbers which are divided by 9 leave a
remainder 6 are 24, 33, 42, 51,...,1104 ...(i)
Numbers which are divided by 21 leaves a
remainder 12 are 33, 54, ...,1104
...(ii)
пҒң
Total numbers are the common number of
terms of sequence (i) and (ii).

Now let there are 'n' common terms of (i) and
(ii)  have a common difference of 63 starting
from number 33 (вҲө L.C.M. of 9 and 21 is 63)
пҒң 33 + (n вҖ“1) Г— 63 = 1104
пғһ
18
пҖҪ
n
пҒң Option (a) is correct.
questions.subject: maths

questions.body: Two unbiased dice are rolled. What is the probability of getting a sum which is neither 7 nor 11?
questions.solution:
Total number of outcomes = 6 Г— 6 = 36
Outcomes whose sum is either 7 or 11 =
(1, 6) (2, 5), (3, 4), (4, 3), (5, 2), (6,1), (5, 6),
(6, 5)
Number of outcomes whose sum is neither
7 nor 11 = 36 вҖ“ 8 = 28
пҒң Required probability = 28
7
36
9
пҖҪ
Hence, option (a) is correct.
questions.subject: maths

questions.body: If sin2x + sin2y + sin2z = 0, then which of the following is NOT a possible value of cosx + cosy + cosz?
questions.solution:
вҲө
sin2x + sin2y + sin2z = 0
пғһ
sinx = siny = sinz = 0
пҒң
cosx = Вұ 1; cosy = Вұ 1; cosz = Вұ 1
пҒң

= 3
or 1 + 1 вҖ“1 = 1 or вҖ“1 вҖ“1 + 1 = вҖ“1
or вҖ“1 вҖ“1 вҖ“1 = вҖ“3
пҒң
cosx + cosy + cosz пӮ№ вҖ“2
пҒң
Option (d) is correct.

questions.body: Find the remainder when x51 is divided by x2 вҖ“ 3x + 2.
questions.solution:
x2 вҖ“ 3x + 2 = (x вҖ“ 1)(x вҖ“ 2)
пҒң
x51 = (x вҖ“ 1)(x вҖ“ 2) Q(x) + (px + q)
Put x = 1 пғһ 1 = (p + q)
...(i)
Put x = 2 пғһ 251 = (2p + q)
...(ii)
Solving equation (i) and (ii), we get
p = (251 вҖ“ 1) and q = 2 вҖ“ 251
пҒң
Remainder = (px + q)
= (251 вҖ“ 1)x + (2 вҖ“ 251)
Option (c) is correct.

questions.body: In case of mice coat colour, two genes are responsible for colour of the hair. Gene вҖҳAвҖҷ is responsible for distribution of pigments on shaft of hair. Wild type allele of вҖҳAвҖҷ produces a yellow band on dark hair shaft (agouti), whereas recessive allele produces no yellow band. There is another allele of A, known as AY, which is embryonic lethal in homozygous condition only. In an experiment, two yellow mice were crossed to obtain a progeny of 6 pups. What would be the most probable number of agouti mice among them?
questions.solution:
In this experiment, three cases are possible
(i) Agouti Г— Agouti = All Agouti
(ii) Yellow Г— Yellow = 2
3 Yellow, 1
3 Agouti
(iii) Agouti Г— Yellow = 1
2 Yellow, 1
2 Agouti
According to the given statement in question,
i.e.,Yellow Г— Yellow
that mean 2
3 Yellow and 1
3 Agouti
So, most probable number of agouti mice
would be = 
1
6
2
3
пӮҙ
пҖҪ
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: A stain was developed by a group of scientists to stain a particular cell organelle. The stain was tested on various tissues derived from an autopsy sample from a mammal. The organelles were counted. The results showed maximum number of the organelles in cells of brain, lesser in cells of heart, least in mature sperms and absent in erythrocytes. Identify the organelles from following options.
questions.solution:
Mitochondria, Golgi bodies and Endoplasmic
reticulum are the organelles absent in
enucleated 
erythrocytes 
of 
mammals
whereas Nissl's bodies are present in neural
tissue which is maximally concentrated in
brain.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Pinus sylvestris grows at low temperatures in Russia. The plant survives under such freezing conditions due to the presence of :
questions.solution:
Pinus sylvestris grows at low temperatures in
Russia. The plant survives under such freezing
conditions 
due 
to 
the 
presence 
of
polyunsaturated lipids in plasma membrane
as these lipids have a lower melting point and
more flexibility than the phospholipids with
saturated acyl chains.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: In an experimental setup, certain pathogen caused a disease in primates with nasal congestion, sore throat and fever being the common symptoms. The scientists injected an extract from blue-green mold as the first line of action. However, the symptoms did not subside. The possible causative agents of the disease were listed out as follows. i. A virus ii. A fungus iii. A conjugation deficient bacterium iv. A tapeworm Choose the correct option from the following that indicate the pathogen.
questions.solution:
Administration of extract from blue-green
mold (fungi) did not subside the symptoms
possibly because the causative agents of the
disease may be virus, bacteria or helminth but
not fungus. So, the most appropriate answer
is option (b).
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: A group of students was studying development of an organism under controlled laboratory conditions. Following observations were made by them. i. The larvae had a rod-like supporting structure that separated the nervous system and the gut. ii. A prominent central cavity was present in the transverse section of the part of the nervous system of the larvae; while the adults had cerebral ganglia as the main component of the nervous system. iii. The eyes were prominently seen in larvae. iv. The tails were absent in the adults, which the larvae had. v. A lot of phagocytic activity was observed before conversion of larvae into adults. vi. The adults had a cuticular exoskeleton. The organism under study must be belonging to:
questions.solution:
вҖңArthropods are non-chordates i.e. lack
notochord which is a rod-like supporting
structure that separates nervous system and
gut as in case of chordates such as
Amphibians, fishes and Protochordates. Adult
Amphibians and fishes have a well developed
nervous system while adult protochordates
had cerebral ganglia as nervous system.
Although both amphibians and protochordates
exhibit вҖҳtadpoleвҖҷ larval stage with prominent
eyes and tails but exoskeleton is present only
is protochordates (tunicates). Therefore,
answer is protochordates.вҖқ
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: A process is represented in the adjacent figure. The arrows indicate the flow of a biochemical reaction. The arrowhead points to the product, while the base of the arrow indicates the template biomolecule. What do P, Q, R, and S represent? X Y Z Q P S R

choices.a:
P 
: 
Replication, 
Q 
: 
Translation,
R : Transcription, S : Reverse Transcription
choices.b:
P : Transcription, Q : Replication,
R : Reverse Transcription, S : Translation
choices.c:
P : Reverse Transcription, Q : Replication,
R : Translation, S : Transcription
choices.d:
P : Reverse Transcription, Q : Replication,
R : Transcription, S : Translation

questions.solution:
RNA (X)
DNA (Y)
Translation
(S)
Replication
(Q)
Proteins (Z)
Transcription (R)
Reverse (P)
Transcription
Here, вҖҳYвҖҷ represents DNA which produces its
new copies by the process of вҖҳReplicationвҖҷ
represented by вҖҳQвҖҷ. RNA is formed from DNA
by the process called вҖҳTranscriptionвҖҷ which is
here represented by вҖҳRвҖҷ. RNA are translated
into proteins by the process called
вҖҳTranslationвҖҷ, represented by вҖҳSвҖҷ here.
In certain viruses, DNA can be synthesize
from RNA by the process of вҖҳReverse
transcriptionвҖҷ, represented by вҖҳSвҖҷ.
Therefore, P is reverse transcription
Q is replication
R is transcription
and
S is translation
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: The whooping cranes were on the verge of extinction with only 21 individuals in wild in 1941. After conservation measures, the cranes are now included in the endangered category by IUCN. The highlight of the conservation efforts is the reintroduction of the whooping cranes in wild. This was possible due to raising of the young cranes in absence of their parents by biologists dressed in crane costumes. Aircraft Guided bird migration technique was used for teaching the captive-bred cranes to follow the scientists to learn the migratory route. What type of animal behaviour might be responsible for these captive-bred cranes to follow the crane costume dressed scientists?
questions.solution:
The type of animal behaviour responsible for
these captiveвҖ“bred cranes to the crane
costume dressed scientists is genetic
imprinting which is a rapid learning process
that takes place early in the life of a social
animal and establishes a behaviour pattern
such as recognition of and attraction to its
own kind or a substitute.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: In the baking industry, when the dough is prepared, various ingredients are mixed together with the flour. At one instance, the dough was fermented, but failed to rise sufficiently during the baking process. Choose the correct cause(s) from following possibilities. i. The salt was mixed before the fermentation process was completed ii. The sugar was added in excess iii. Yeast granules were not activated prior to mixing with the flour.
questions.solution:
Addition of salt in the dough slows down
fermentation and enzyme activity in dough.
Excess sugar in dough can slow down or even
inhibit yeast activity. As sugar level increases,
yeast becomes stressed as less water is
available for it to function. Inactivated yeast
granules consist of yeast cells that are no
longer living or active and reduces mix time
and creates a more extensible dough once
they are activated.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Given below are four statements. I. Prokaryotic cells are unicellular while eukaryotes are multicellular. II. Histones are present in eukaryotes and absent in prokaryotes. III. The nucleoid contains the genetic material in prokaryotes and eukaryotes. IV. Prokaryotic flagellum is composed of flagellin while eukaryotic flagellum is composed of tubulin. Identify which amongst these are false.
questions.solution:
All eukaryotes are not multicellular. For
example, 
protistans 
are 
unicellular
eukaryotes. The region that contains genetic
material in prokaryotes is called nucleoid
whereas in eukaryotes it is called nucleus.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: The students of a college were working on regeneration using Planaria (Platyhelminthes) and Asterias (Echinodermata). Planaria was cut in three pieces, namely, a piece with head, with tail and the middle piece. Asterias (bearing five arms) was cut in such a way that after separation, six pieces were obtained, namely, an arm with a portion of the central disc, four pieces cut from tips of each of the remaining arms and the remaining body. The animals were allowed to regenerate completely. How many Planaria and Asterias respectively will be obtained after the completion of regeneration in both?
questions.solution:
Planarians possess a tremendous power of
regeneration. If cut across into three parts,
each part regenerates into a complete normal
individual.
In case of Asterias, body part with the portion
of central disc can regenerate an entire
animal four pieces cut from tips of each of the
remaining arms are devoid of central disc,
hence, cannot regenerate while the remaining
body with remaining central disc can
regenerate an entire animal.
Therefore, 
after 
the 
completion 
of
regeneration in Asterias total two organisms
will be obtained.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Fecundity in animal world is the maximum possible ability of an individual to produce offsprings during its entire lifetime. Following factors were checked for their effect on fecundity of different animal models. i. Availability of food during breeding season ii. Mode of fertilization iii. Population density Which of these factors(s) can regulate fecundity?
questions.solution:
All the given factors i.e. the availability of food
during breeding season, mode of fertilization
and population density affect the fecundity of
animals.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: An organism has 27 pairs of homologous chromosomes. In each daughter cell after completion of mitosis and in each gamete after completion of meiosis II, ________ and _________ chromosomes would be present respectively.
questions.solution:
In mitosis, mother cell divides to form two
identical daughter cells which have the same
number of chromosomes as mother cell.
Hence, each daughter cell would have
27 pairs or 54 chromosomes.
Meiosis is a reductional division which
involves two consecutive divisions. The
daughter cells have half the number of
chromosomes than that of mother cell.
Hence, in each gamete, after completion of
meiosis-II, 27 chromosomes would be present.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Rahul sprayed a chemical вҖҳXвҖҷ on a plant with rosette habit. After few days, he found the internodal distances to have increased suddenly. The chemical вҖҳXвҖҷ might be:
questions.solution:
The sprayed chemical 'X' is Gibberellic acid
which induces internodal and stem elongation
in rosette plants.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: In case of peppered moths, pale and dark moths are observed. Pale variety is known to be the wild type variety. During industrial revolution, industrial melanism led to prevalence of dark variety around the cities and pale variety continued to be in majority in areas away from the industries. After enforcement of regulations for controlling pollution, reappearance of pale moths in majority was observed around cities again. Driving force(s) for these adaptive changes is/ are: i. Increased pollution around industries ii. A stable transposition of a gene in moths iii. Limitations of the vision of birds to differentiate dark moths on darkened barks and pale moths in presence of lichens iv. Ability of lichens to grow on barks in less polluted areas only.
questions.solution:
Pre-Industrial Period
Post-Industrial Period
вҖў
вҖў
No air pollution
Pollution increased
вҖў
вҖў
Tree trunk lighter
in colour
Tree trunk darken
вҖў
вҖў
No smoke deposition
and thick growth of
white coloured
lichens on tree trunk
Smoke and soot deposition
on trunk preventing lichens
growth
вҖў
вҖў
Less predation of
pale moth
Dark moths are not visible
while pale moths cannot
camouflage causing
limitations of vision of birds
leading more predation of
pale moths
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: A 4 пҒӯm long bacterial cell was magnified and drawn to a dimension of 6 cm. How many times has it been magnified?
questions.solution:
Size of bacterial cell = 4 пҒӯm = 4 Г— 10вҖ“6 m
Size of drawn cell = 6 cm = 6 Г— 10вҖ“2 m
So, bacterial cell magnification = 
пҖӯ
пӮҙ
пӮҙ
2
6
6 10
4 10
= 1.5 Г— 104
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Gymnosperms are called вҖҳnaked seed bearing plantsвҖҷ because they lack:
questions.solution:
Gymnosperms are called вҖҳnaked seeds
bearing plantsвҖҷ because they lack ovary. The
seeds are not enclosed within the fruit.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Any damage or injury to a particular area causes nociceptors to release some chemicals, which carry the signal to the higher centres in the nervous system for the processing and a subsequent action. However, there is a difference in the way in which the stimulus is received which is related to the acuity of the detection. Fingertips are more sensitive as compared to the forearm. Following reasons for the observed phenomenon were suggested. i. The receptive fields in the fingertip are smaller ii. The number of nociceptors per receptive field in the forearm is lesser iii. The amount of prostaglandins released by the nociceptors per receptive field is more in fingertips The most probable reason(s) for this may be:
questions.solution:
In fingertips, the receptive fields are smaller
and densely packed with nociceptors or pain
receptors. The number of nociceptors and
amount of prostaglandins released by these
receptors per receptive field is also more in
fingertips in comparison to forearm.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Rate of Photosynthesys Parameter Rate of photosynthesis in hydrophytes depends on various parameters. The adjacent graph shows the effect of one parameter (while keeping all the others constant) on the rate of photosynthesis. Rate of photosynthesis is plotted on Y axis. Identify the parameter which is plotted along X axis:
questions.solution:
Light between 400-700 nm wavelength
constitute the photosynthetically active
radiation. Maximum photosynthesis takes
place in red and blue light of the visible
spectrum and minimum photosynthesis takes
place in green light.
Rate of Photosynthesys
400
500

questions.skip_reason: Question references a graph that we cannot see

questions.body: On a study tour, plants with leathery leaves with thick cuticle, vivipary, salt glands, apogeotropic roots and stomata limited to abaxial surface were observed. The plants might be:
questions.solution:
Mangroves are adapted to grow in a wet and
salty place. They have leathery leaves with
thick cuticle, show vivipary (the young plant
within the seed), have salt glands, have
apogeotropic roots (roots that grow away
from the ground) and have stomata limited to
abaxial surface.
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: Four different human body fluid samples were subjected to quantification of hydrogen ion concentration. mEq/L is the unit of measurement for hydrogen ion concentration. The results of the experiment were as follows: Sample A: 1.6 Г— 102 units Sample B: 4.5 Г— 10вҖ“5 units Sample C: 1 Г— 10вҖ“3 units Sample D: 3 Г— 10вҖ“2 units Identify the samples in sequence from A to D.

choices.a:
Gastric HCI, Venous blood, Intracellular
Fluid, Urine
choices.b:
Venous blood, Intracellular Fluid, Gastric
HCI, Urine
choices.c:
Urine, Gastric HCI, Venous blood,
Intracellular Fluid
choices.d:
Intracellular Fluid, Urine, Gastric HCI,
Venous blood

questions.solution:
Higher the amount of hydrogen ions, lower
will be the pH of body fluid.
On this basis we can conclude that
Sample A with maximum hydrogen ions
concentration facilitate lowest pH among the
given samples indicating that sample A is
gastric HCl.

questions.subject: chemistry

questions.body: Which of the following polymeric material will be ideal for remoulding?
questions.solution:
Polyvinyl chloride and polythene are
thermoplastics and therefore they are ideal
for remoulding.
questions.subject: chemistry

questions.body: An element Y is a white translucent solid at room temperature and exhibits various allotropic forms. Some compounds of element Y find application in agricultural industry. Y forms two solid oxides which dissolve in water to form comparatively weak acids. The element Y is:
questions.solution:
Oxides of carbon, sulphur and nitrogen are
gases, whereas oxides of phosphorus i.e. P4O6
and P4O10 are solids and react with water to
give weak acids.
Therefore, element вҖҳYвҖҷ is phosphorus.
questions.subject: chemistry

questions.body: How many sigma bonds are present between any two carbon atoms in fullerenes?
questions.solution:
In fullerenes, one sigma bond is present
between any two carbon atoms.
questions.subject: chemistry

questions.body: A student was studying reactions of metals with dilute NaOH at room temperature. The student took dilute NaOH in four different test tubes and added copper powder to test tube A, zinc dust to test tube B, aluminium powder to test tube C and iron powder to test tube D and observed effervescence in _______.
questions.solution:
In test tube 'B' and 'C' effervescence will be
observed due to the evolution of hydrogen
gas, whereas in test tube 'A' and 'D', no such
effervescence will be observed.
questions.subject: chemistry

questions.body: A magician performed following act: He dipped Rs. 50 note in a 50% solution of alcohol in water and held it on the burning flame, but the note did not burn. The reason behind this is

choices.a: The alcohol kept on dousing the fire
choices.b: Air required for burning was not available
choices.c:
The Rs. 50 note failed to reach ignition
temperature
choices.d: The Rs. 50 note is fire proof

questions.solution:
The heat supplied to the Rs. 50 note is
transferred to water by conduction. So, in the
presence of 50% solution of alcohol in water,
the ignition temperature of paper is not
reached. Hence, it does not burn.
questions.subject: chemistry

questions.body: Substance X is white crystalline solid which melts after 10 seconds on burner flame. It is soluble in water and insoluble in CCl4. It is a poor conductor of electricity in molten state as well as in the form of aqueous solution, hence we coclude that substance X is
questions.solution:
Since substance X melts after 10 seconds on
burner flame and is a poor conductor of
electricity in molten state as well as in the
form of aqueous solution, this means 'X' is a
covalent compound. Its solubility in water
indicates that it is polar in nature.
пҒң
'X' is a polar covalent compound.
questions.subject: chemistry

questions.body: In a beaker 50 ml of a normal HCl solution was taken and NH3 gas was passed through it for some time. The contents of the beaker were then titrated, which required 60 ml of semi normal NaOH solution. How much ammonia was passed through the beaker?
questions.solution:
Milliequivalent of NaOH = NVml
= 0.5 Г— 60
= 30
Milliequivalent of HCl = NVml = 1 Г— 50 = 50
In neutralisation reaction;
Total milliequivalent of HCl = Milliequilvalent
of NH3 +
Milliequivalent of
NaOH
50 = Milliequivalent of NH3 + 30
Milliequivalent of NH3 = 50 вҖ“ 30 = 20
Moles of NH3 = 20 Г— 10вҖ“3 moles
Mass of NH3 = 20 Г— 10вҖ“3 Г— 17 = 0.34 g

questions.body: A zinc rod was dipped in 100 cm3 of 1M copper chloride solution. After certain time the molarity of Cu2+ ions in the solution was found to be 0.8 M. If the weight of zinc rod is 20 g, then the molarity of chloride ions is ______.
questions.solution:
When Zn rod is dipped in copper chloride
solution, following reaction takes place:
CuCl2 + Zn пӮ® Cu + ZnCl2
All the chloride ions will remain in solution.
пҒң
Molarity of ClвҖ“ ions = 2 Г— 1 = 2 M.

questions.body: Which of the following are not ionic? (i) AlCl3 (ii) CaCl2 (iii) MgCl2 (iv) LiCl
questions.solution:
Chlorides of aluminium and lithium are
predominantely covalent in nature.
